title: Headings and paragraphs
description: Structure text with h1-h6 and p.

questions.prompt: How many <h1> elements should a well-structured page have?
questions.explanation: One <h1> per document is the recommended and most accessible pattern.

questions.prompt: Which heading level represents a subsection of an <h2>?
questions.explanation: Levels are hierarchical: h1 > h2 > h3 > ...

questions.prompt: Which of these is bad practice?
questions.code:
A)

Site

...

Section

B)

Site

...

Section

C)

Site

...

Section

Sub

D)

Sub

inside a section
questions.explanation: Skipping heading levels (h1 → h4) breaks the document outline.

questions.prompt: Which HTML tag makes text a NEW paragraph?
For a line break WITHIN a paragraph:
questions.explanation: is a void element for hard breaks (addresses, poetry).

questions.prompt: Which is a THEMATIC break (topic change)?
questions.explanation: <hr> is semantically a thematic break (renders as a horizontal rule).

questions.prompt: How is this rendered by a screen reader?
options: Both announced as top-level headings — bad structure
questions.explanation: Multiple h1s hurt the document outline. Prefer ONE h1 per document.
h6 is the deepest (smallest by default). Use only if you actually go that deep.
